answer:
**Iqbal's "Tanhai" (Solitude)** from *Bang-E-Dara Part 2* portrays solitude as a **chosen, harmonious state**, where the natural world—stars, sky, earth, moon, desert—serves as kindred companions to the heart, transforming potential sorrow into kinship and wonder.[^1] The poem questions any grief in isolation, reframing tears as stars and nature as a "wild rose garden," with the heart's longing affirmed as a positive bond: "What is this longing you cannot remit, O heart! / For Nature is your kindred spirit, O heart!"[^1]

**Ghalib's ghazal** (ch. 67, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*) evokes **inflicted solitude** as tormenting isolation, tied to separation (*furqat*), burning inner fire, fleeing shadows, and dread in "lonely nights," where the self's strain amplifies distance from the beloved and goal.[^2] Lines like "In lonely nights, my heart's fire burns with such a strain from me, / That like a wisp of smoke, my shadow flees in pain from me" and "Such lonely dread on nights of parting, a wild strain from me" underscore pain, not embrace.[^2]

### Staged Conversation: Chosen vs. Inflicted Solitude
*(Voices drawn directly from the poems' verses, personified as Iqbal and Ghalib debating in a moonlit desert—nature's garden meets the lover's burning waste.)*

**Iqbal:** Are the stars sorrowful in the night? Are they not your companions![^3]  
This lofty, silent sky so deep, the slumbering earth, the world asleep.  
This moon, the desert, vale, and mountain-bar, all Nature is a wild rose garden, near and far.[^4]

**Ghalib:** With every step, the goal recedes in vain from me; my very speed makes the whole desert plain flee from me.[^5]  
In lonely nights, my heart's fire burns with such a strain from me, that like a wisp of smoke, my shadow flees in pain from me.[^6] Such lonely dread on nights of parting, a wild strain from me; my shadow hides inside the Judgment sun, to wane, from me.[^7]

**Iqbal:** These lovely pearls, so bright they are, are but your tears, become a star. What is this longing you cannot remit, O heart! For Nature is your kindred spirit, O heart![^8]

**Ghalib:** May idols never learn the grief that lovers drain from me; the house of mirrors stands an empty, vast domain from me.[^9]  
If self-loss were a bed of peace, I'd not abstain from me; my private room is filled, a shadow's dark domain, from me.[^10]

**Iqbal:** (reprise) Are the stars sorrowful...? Nature awaits—embrace it![^3]

**Ghalib:** The path of madness, by my blisters, burns again from me... To light the fire with just one hint—that single spark is from you; to set a hundred cities blazing in the dark is from me.[^11]

*(Iqbal's chosen solitude blooms in nature's embrace; Ghalib's inflicted isolation scorches with unquenched fire. The desert holds both.)*

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 25 (*Tanhai*), [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/25-tanhai/translation/).
[^2]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 67 (*Har qadam doori-e-manzil hai numayan mujh se*), [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/).
[^3]: Ibid., [p-e79836](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/25-tanhai/translation/#p-e79836).
[^4]: Ibid., [p-471a1d](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/25-tanhai/translation/#p-471a1d).
[^5]: Ghalib, ch. 67, [p-08026e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/#p-08026e).
[^6]: Ibid., [p-2e3269](https://falsafa.corpus/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/#p-2e3269).
[^7]: Ibid., [p-aa1fc0](https://falsafa.corpus/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/#p-aa1fc0).
[^8]: Iqbal, ch. 25, [p-8846ae](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/25-tanhai/translation/#p-8846ae).
[^9]: Ghalib, ch. 67, [p-85ee45](https://falsafa.corpus/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/#p-85ee45).
[^10]: Ibid., [p-263664](https://falsafa.corpus/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/#p-263664).
[^11]: Ibid., [p-fc4381](https://falsafa.corpus/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/#p-fc4381) and [p-09aa86](https://falsafa.corpus/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/#p-09aa86).
